title: Microsoft Graph - Teams API: Channel Messages, Adaptive Cards, And Security Alerting
applies_to: Posting security alerts and pipeline status updates to Teams channels
Sending Adaptive Card formatted notifications to Teams from automation scripts
Teams team and channel provisioning via Graph API

confirmed_facts.fact: Post a message to a Teams channel: `POST /teams/{teamId}/channels/{channelId}/messages` with body `{ "body": { "contentType": "html", "content": "Security Alert: Critical finding detected." } }`. Requires `ChannelMessage.Send` delegated permission or `Teamwork.Migrate.All` for app-only (migration scenario). For standard app-only posting, use a channel webhook or incoming webhook connector instead.

Incoming webhooks (Teams connector): create an Incoming Webhook in Teams channel settings > Connectors > Incoming Webhook. Post to the webhook URL via HTTP POST with a JSON body. Supports legacy Office 365 connectors format and Adaptive Cards via `type: 'message'` payload. Incoming webhooks do not require any Entra ID permissions — the URL is the authentication token.

Post an Adaptive Card to Teams via incoming webhook: POST to the webhook URL with `{ "type": "message", "attachments": [{ "contentType": "application/vnd.microsoft.card.adaptive", "content": { "$schema": "http://adaptivecards.io/schemas/adaptive-card.json", "type": "AdaptiveCard", "version": "1.4", "body": [...] } }] }`. Adaptive Cards support rich formatting: ColumnSets, FactSets, Images, and Action buttons.

PowerShell post to Teams webhook: `$body = @{ type='message'; attachments=@(@{ contentType='application/vnd.microsoft.card.adaptive'; content=$adaptiveCard }) } | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 10; Invoke-RestMethod -Uri $webhookUrl -Method POST -Body $body -ContentType 'application/json'`. No authentication header required — the webhook URL is self-authenticating.

List teams and channels via Graph: `GET /teams` (requires Team.ReadBasic.All), `GET /teams/{teamId}/channels` (returns all channels). Filter standard channels: `$filter=membershipType eq 'standard'`. Get a specific channel by name: `GET /teams/{teamId}/channels?$filter=displayName eq 'Security Alerts'`.

Create a new team: `POST /teams` with body including `displayName`, `description`, `template@odata.bind` (e.g., `https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/teamsTemplates('standard')`), and `members` array. Team creation is asynchronous — the response is a 202 Accepted with an operation location header. Poll `GET /teamsAsyncOperations/{operationId}` until status is `succeeded`.

implementation_notes: For the security reporting pipeline, use an incoming webhook to post a summary card to a 'Security Reports' Teams channel at the end of each pipeline run. Include: run status (success/failure), record counts per source, and a link to the Power BI report. This provides instant visibility without requiring report consumers to check Power BI directly.

Store the webhook URL in Key Vault or an Automation encrypted variable — while it does not require Entra auth, it is a sensitive credential that grants anyone with the URL the ability to post to the channel.

For alert-based notifications (e.g., BitSight rating drops below threshold), use a Logic App webhook trigger or Azure Function that fires on the alert and posts an Adaptive Card with the company name, old/new rating, and a link to the BitSight portal.
Use FactSet in Adaptive Cards to display key-value pairs neatly: `{ "type": "FactSet", "facts": [{ "title": "Qualys Critical", "value": "14" }, { "title": "BitSight Avg", "value": "720" }] }`.
caveats: Graph API app-only channel message posting (`ChannelMessage.Send` as application permission) requires the app to be installed in the team. For simple automation notifications, incoming webhooks are simpler and do not require app installation.

Incoming webhooks are being transitioned by Microsoft to Workflows (Power Automate) in newer Teams tenants. Verify that legacy incoming webhook connectors are still enabled in your tenant's Teams admin settings.
Teams webhook URL rotation: if a webhook URL is compromised, delete and recreate the connector in Teams. Update the stored URL in Key Vault immediately.
